$schema_note: Eval-case definitions for rai-orm-from-text, v0.2. Mirror of the schema-skill's cases.json structure. Claude reads this in-session, runs each case, and compares against expected.
v0_2_pass_bar: T-E1 must pass on the synthetic_good_user dialogue + (future work) 3 paraphrased Halpin Workbook exercises; T-E2 coverage targets met per exercise; T-E3 and T-E4 pass on manual review; T-E5 pass with at least one stakeholder.
future_fixtures_pending: fixtures/workbook_exercise_1/dialogue.md (paraphrased from Halpin Workbook per S12 copyright; not yet authored)

cases.description: Synthetic dialogue with a well-engaged user (provides examples, answers clarifying questions, distinguishes alethic from deontic).
Count constraints by source category per exercise; verify each category meets its target.

targets.explicit: ≥ 60% of total constraints (CSDP is dialogue-driven; explicit constraints from user statements dominate)
targets.sample: ≥ 1 constraint per exercise (small Halpin populations should yield at least one)
targets.common-sense: ≥ 40% of expected count (library hits on common object-type names)
targets.llm-inferred: ≥ 1 proposal per exercise with rationale_world_fact populated; ≥ 50% rejected at 6d/7b (LLM is highest-hallucination-risk source)
targets.user-supplied: ≥ 1 per exercise (CSDP Step 7d should typically surface at least one missed rule)

cases.description: Manual review of Claude's question-asking quality at ambiguous CSDP steps.
review_criteria: Did Claude elicit reference schemes explicitly (vs assuming attribute thinking)? Especially: was each new entity type's reference mode chosen by an explicit user decision?
Did Claude verbalize each fact as a question, never as an assertion?
Did Claude push back on premature subtyping using the Halpin role-based criterion?
Did Claude decompose compound predicates into elementary facts?
Did Claude redirect ER vocabulary (table/column/attribute) to Halpin terms (fact type/role/object type)?
cases.pass: All five criteria pass on manual review across all T-E1 cases.

cases.description: Manual review of Halpin-posture adherence — terminology, atomic decomposition, fact-based thinking.
review_criteria: All emitted YAML uses Halpin vocabulary (fact_type, object_type, reference mode, role, etc.) — never ER vocabulary (table, column, attribute, relationship type, junction)
All fact types are atomic (no compound predicates)
No subtypes are emitted whose justification is attribute-based rather than role-based
Sample populations rendered in the verbalization (when present in YAML)
User quotes cited in provenance for source=explicit and source=user-supplied constraints

cases.description: Hand the verbalization to a non-PyRel stakeholder; can they validate the model by reading verbalizations?
cases.method: Run CSDP on one of the T-E1 fixtures. Hand the resulting verbalization.txt to a domain expert who is NOT familiar with PyRel, ORM 2 notation, or Halpin's textbook. Have them fill in evals/reviewability_checklist.md.
cases.pass: At least one stakeholder confirms they can validate the model without help (no need to see the YAML or original schema/dialogue).

running_evals.manual_methodology: Claude runs CSDP on each fixture in-session, writes outputs to evals/results/<YYYY-MM-DD>/<exercise>/{output.orm.yaml, output.py, output.verbalization.txt, diff.md}, then produces a per-case verdict comparing against the expected reference and the pass criteria.
running_evals.diff_format: diff.md per run: (1) T-E1 structural diff, (2) T-E2 source-category counts vs targets, (3) T-E3 question-asking review with concrete examples, (4) T-E4 posture-adherence review, (5) overall pass/fail.
running_evals.automation_status: v0.2: manual. Stretch goal — eval runner if N grows.
